text: ## Page 163 ## Page 164 ## Page 165 **5.6** ## Page 165 # **Contribution to the decarbonisation of transport** ## Page 165 **CIE Automotive's business is making a decisive contribution to the decarbonisation of road transport insofar as its parts are being incorporated into zeroemission vehicles. As a result, CIE Automotive's business qualifies as eligible for EU Taxonomy purposes as it is a priority for the region's the climate mitigation and climate change adaptation objectives.** ## Page 165 Image /page/164/Picture/14 description: The image shows the CIE Automotive logo. The logo consists of a stylized letter C on the left and the text "CIE Automotive" on the right. The letter C is made up of two curved shapes, one above the other, that do not quite meet in the middle. The text "CIE Automotive" is in a simple, sans-serif font. ## Page 165 Chairman and CEO's [statement](#page-2-0) ## Page 165 **2023 [highlights](#page-5-0) 1** ## Page 165 **Ready** Ready **for change 2** ## Page 165 **Our response to the environmental 5** challenge **challenge** ## Page 165 **Record [earnings](#page-53-0) 3** ## Page 165 **Common responsibility, [shared benefits](#page-64-0) 4** ## Page 165 > About [this report](#page-229-0) ## Page 165 **Responsible [management](#page-183-0) 6** ## Page 165 [Data tables](#page-261-0) ## Page 165 Image /page/164/Picture/0 description: The image shows the words "ANNUAL REPORT" on the left side of the image. On the right side of the image is the number 23. ## Page 166 The EU Taxonomy is a classification system establishing the conditions that an economic activity has to meet in order to qualify as environmentally sustainable; specifically an activity must make a substantial contribution to one or more of the six environmental objectives established by the European Union, without having a significant detrimental impact (the Do No Significant Harm principle) on the other five, while meeting certain minimum social safeguards, defined as ILO Core Labour Conventions, the OECD Guidelines for Multinational Enterprises and the UN Guiding Principles on Business and Human Rights. ## Page 166 The ultimate goal of the Taxonomy is to redirect investment flows towards climate neutrality (net zero GHG emissions) via a two-pronged plan: helping players in the financial sector to decarbonise their securities and credit portfolios and giving non-financial companies (such as CIE Automotive) increased access to financing for technology, products and services in line with the guidelines set in the green transition framework. ## Page 166 Following an analysis of its activities, CIE Automotive concluded that its productive processes, despite applying a range of methodologies, are encompassed by economic activity code NACE C29.3.2. Manufacture of other parts and accessories for motor vehicles. ## Page 166 That economic activity is identified in Commission Delegated Regulation 2023/2485 of 27 June 2023, within the climate change mitigation objective, under code **3.18. Manufacture of automotive and mobility components**, which is defined as follows: ## Page 166 *Manufacture, repair, maintenance, retrofitting, repurposing and upgrade of mobility components for zeroemission personal mobility devices and of automotive and mobility systems, components, separate technical units, parts and spare parts as defined in Article 3, points (18) to (21) and (23) of Regulation (EU) 2018/858 of the European Parliament and of the Council, type approved, designed, and constructed for use only in vehicles and buses of category M1, M2, M3, N1, N2 and N3, and in Article 3, points (15) to (18) and (21) in Regulation (EU) 168/2013 of the European Parliament and of the Council, type approved, designed, and constructed for use only in vehicles of category L meeting the criteria set out in this Section and which are essential for delivering and improving the environmental performance of the vehicle.* ## Page 166 As a result, as defined in the Regulation, CIE Automotive's economic activity is Taxonomyeligible in 2023. Given that this activity is being reported for the first time this year, as prescribed in Article 5 of Commission ## Page 166 Delegated Regulation 2023/2485 of 27 June 2023, this year the Group only has to report eligibility; the alignment disclosures will be mandatory next year. ## Page 166 In addition, in order to comply with the Regulation's requirements, the Group assessed the materiality of the three KPIs - turnover, CapEx and OPEX - in relation to the rest of the activities described in the Regulations, as outlined next. ## Page 166 #### **TURNOVER** ## Page 166 The key performance indicator (KPI) related to turnover is calculated as the percentage of turnover derived from Taxonomy-aligned activities (numerator) over total company turnover (denominator). Turnover is defined as the revenue recognised pursuant to International Accounting Standard (IAS) 1, paragraph 82(a), as adopted by Commission Regulation (EC) No 1126/2008. ## Page 166 CIE Automotive, S.A. reports an eligible turnover KPI associated with activity 3.18 equivalent to 13.4% (turnover of 531,588 thousand euros). This percentage is arrived at by assigning each automotive part made by each of our factories to each of the original equipment makers that exist in the world. To obtain the percentage of electric vehicles made by all the platforms, CIE Automotive relied on information reported by IHS Markit, one of the largest data and analytics providers for many industries, with a solid presence in the automotive sector. IHS Markit provides the percentages of pure electric vehicles manufactured globally by each platform. CIE Automotive uses those percentages and the sales figures by platform as the basis for estimating turnover associated with zeroemissions vehicles.

## Page 166 ### **COMPLIANCE WITH REGULATION (EU) 2020/852 ON CLIMATE TAXONOMY** Chairman ## Page 166 and CEO's [statement](#page-2-0) ## Page 166 **2023 [highlights](#page-5-0) 1** ## Page 166 **Ready [for change](#page-15-0) 2** ## Page 166 **Record [earnings](#page-53-0) Common responsibility, [shared benefits](#page-64-0) 3 4** ## Page 166 #### **Our response to [the environmental](#page-141-0) challenge 5** ## Page 166 About [this report](#page-229-0) ## Page 166 **Responsible [management](#page-183-0) 6** ## Page 166 [Data tables](#page-261-0) ## Page 166 Image /page/165/Picture/20 description: The image shows the logo for CIE Automotive. The logo consists of a stylized letter "C" on the left, followed by the text "CIE Automotive" on the right. The "C" is formed by two curved black shapes that do not quite meet, creating an opening. The text "CIE Automotive" is in a simple, sans-serif font, with "CIE" in a bolder typeface than "Automotive". ## Page 166 Image /page/165/Picture/0 description: The image shows the words "ANNUAL REPORT" in bold, black letters. To the right of the words is a large gray quotation mark and the number 23 in black. ## Page 167 #### **CapEx** ## Page 167 The CapEx KPI is measured as the percentage of fixed assets invested in eligible economic activities (numerator) over total assets acquired in the course of 2023 (denominator). That denominator (Total CapEx) is defined as additions to tangible and intangible assets during the financial year considered before depreciation, amortisation and any remeasurements, including those resulting from revaluations and impairments, for the relevant financial year and excluding fair value changes. It also includes additions resulting from business combinations. Accordingly, Total CapEx covers the costs recognised in keeping with IAS 16, IAS 38, IAS 40, IAS 41 and IFRS 16. In 2023, the CIE Automotive Group's CapEx totalled 311,884 thousand euros. ## Page 167 With respect to activity 3.18. Manufacture of automotive and mobility components, the Group attempted to estimate how much of its CapEx is associated with that activity. That was not possible, however, since the physical assets used to make a part for use in an electric vehicle (eligible activity) are for the most part the same assets as are used to make parts for use in a non-electric vehicle (non-eligible activity). The company is working to develop and improve its methodology and technological toolkit to enable it to directly identify the CapEx associated directly with the eligible activity. ## Page 167 As for the rest of its CapEx, the Group assessed whether the activities described in the Regulations are activities carried out by CIE Automotive, concluding that the amount of associated CapEx is not material. ## Page 167 Likewise, in an attempt to comply with the Regulation requirements, the Tables include the most significant activities, even though the KPI reported by the company is equivalent to 0%, specifically, activities 7.3 Installation, maintenance and repair of energy efficiency equipment and 7.6 Installation, maintenance and repair of renewable energy technologies, which account for just 323 thousand euros, just 0.1% of the company's Total CapEx. ## Page 167 It was concluded, therefore, that eligible CapEx under the Taxonomy is equivalent to 0%, so that alignment was not assessed for these activities; nevertheless, the eligible figures are provided in the section titled "Data tables and glossary". ## Page 167 ### **OpEx** ## Page 167 The OpEx KPI is defined as the percentage of Taxonomy-eligible OpEx (numerator) over total qualifying OpEx (denominator). That denominator covers direct noncapitalised costs that relate to research and development, building renovation measures, short-term lease, maintenance and repair, and any other direct expenditures relating to the day-to-day servicing of assets of property, plant and equipment by the undertaking or third party to whom activities are outsourced that are necessary to ensure the continued and effective functioning of such assets. ## Page 167 In 2023, the CIE Automotive Group's qualifying OpEx amounted to 60,045 thousand euros, equivalent to 1.7% of the CIE Automotive Group's total OpEx in 2023 (3,567 million euros), so that the Group's qualifying OpEx is considered non-material and, as prescribed in section 1.1.3.2 of Annex I of the Commission Delegated Regulation of 6 July 2021, the numerator is therefore disclosed as being equal to zero, although the denominator is disclosed. ## Page 167 | | Chairman

## Page 167 [this report](#page-229-0) ## Page 167 [Data tables](#page-261-0) ## Page 167 Image /page/166/Picture/15 description: The image shows the logo for CIE Automotive. The logo consists of a stylized letter "C" on the left, followed by the text "CIE Automotive" on the right. The letter "C" is formed by two curved shapes that do not fully connect, creating an incomplete circle. The text "CIE Automotive" is in a simple, sans-serif font. ## Page 167 Image /page/166/Picture/16 description: The image is a light gray illustration of a road winding through a landscape with stylized trees on either side. The road is depicted as a broad, curving path that starts from the lower left corner and extends upwards and towards the right. The trees are simple, rounded shapes with short trunks, giving them a cartoonish appearance. The overall tone of the image is soft and minimalist, with a focus on basic shapes and a muted color palette. ## Page 167 Image /page/166/Picture/0 description: The image shows the words "ANNUAL REPORT" in bold, black letters. To the right of the words is a large number "23" with a gray quotation mark above the number. ## Page 168 **5.7** ## Page 168 # **Environmental risk management** ## Page 168 The risk of accidents is covered by the company's civil liability insurance, while the company underwrites gradual pollution risk for the following factories in Spain: CIE Alcasting (aluminium), CIE Amaya (machining), CIE Galfor (forging), CIE Inyectametal (aluminium) and CIE Vilanova (aluminium). The last three facilities have integrated pollution prevention control (IPPC) permits. The claims limit on that gradual pollution insurance policy for those five factories is €3 million. ## Page 168 To reinforce the management of its environmental and safety risks, CIE Automotive is working on a joint project with the technical department at its insurance broker, which is part of a bigger project addressed in the "Customer satisfaction" section. Specifically, a series of risks are being evaluated and prioritised in accordance with criteria established by the broker's risk engineers with the idea of formulating plans for their elimination, or at least mitigation. In 2023, this project focused on factories in North America, Brazil and India, prioritising the largest facilities or those with most assets involved. There were also follow-up visits to factories already inspected in prior years. ## Page 168 Climate change also poses a threat to the company's ability to attain its strategic targets. To that end, CIE Automotive includes climate change risk in its risk mapping process, addressing not only the risks deriving from the company's impact on the environment and climate change, but also the risks posed to the company by the environment and climate change, and the interrelationship between the two. ## Page 168 Image /page/167/Picture/16 description: The image shows the CIE Automotive logo. The logo consists of a stylized letter "C" on the left and the text "CIE Automotive" on the right. The "C" is made up of two curved black shapes that do not quite meet, forming an incomplete circle. The text "CIE Automotive" is in a simple, sans-serif font, with "CIE" in a bolder weight than "Automotive". The logo is in black and white. ## Page 168 Chairman and CEO's [statement](#page-2-0) ## Page 168 **2023 [highlights](#page-5-0) 1** ## Page 168 **Ready 2 ready for change** ## Page 168 **Record [earnings](#page-53-0) 3** ## Page 168 **Common responsibility, [shared benefits](#page-64-0) 4** ## Page 168 **Our response to [the environmental](#page-141-0) challenge 5** ## Page 168 > About [this report](#page-229-0) ## Page 168 **Responsible [management](#page-183-0) 6** ## Page 168 [Data tables](#page-261-0) ## Page 168 Image /page/167/Picture/1 description: The image shows the words "ANNUAL REPORT" in white on a green background. To the right of the words is a large white number "23" with a quotation mark above it. ## Page 169 #### **RISK ANALYSIS AND TARGET AND SCENARIO GENERATION** ## Page 169 The chart depicts the environmental governance regime, which includes the establishment and implementation of policies, the management of environmental and climate risks, the oversight and execution of measures and the tracking of mitigation plans using indicators and other monitoring tools. ## Page 169 Image /page/168/Figure/3 description: This image is a diagram of a corporate structure. At the top is the "Annual General Meeting" which feeds into the "Board of Directors". The Board of Directors feeds into three committees: "Appointments and Remuneration Committee", "Audit and Compliance Committee", and "ESG Committee (Environmental, Social and Governance)". The ESG Committee and the Appointments and Remuneration Committee feed into the "Chief Executive Officer". The Chief Executive Officer feeds into the "Executive Committee" and the "Cross-Group ESG Committee". The Executive Committee and the Cross-Group ESG Committee feed into "CIE Automotive". CIE Automotive is fed by "Network Services + Business units". The diagram also includes "Authorisation and reporting", "Implementation", "Policy", "Environmental Risks", and "Performance monitoring". The diagram also includes a list of items: "Chairman and CEO's statement", "2023 highlights", "Ready for change", "Record earnings", "Common responsibility, shared benefits", "Our response to the environmental challenge", "Responsible management", "About this report", and "Data tables". The diagram also includes a list of KPIs: "KPIs", "Environmental footprint", "ISO standard", and "Decarbonisation guidelines".

## Page 169

It shows how the regime involves every part of the company, from the company's shareholders to the business units. The details of the system and how it works are set down in the regulations of the Board and its committees, including the ESG Committee, which are available for public consultation on the company's website, within the corporate governance section.

## Page 169

This governance model is the upper layer in the climate risk management model described next.

## Page 170

# Climate risk map

## Page 170

### **RISK MAPPING: PROCEDURE AND SCOPE**

## Page 170

In response to demands from our stakeholders and in line with the 2025 Strategic Plan, in 2023, CIE Automotive updated and broadened its climate risk map, a process which involved a bigger number of its professionals. Specifically, in 2023, the company assessed 12 risks, gathering input from

## Page 170

145 professionals in total.

## Page 170

The climate risk map is integrated within CIE Automotive's enterprise risk management (ERM) system, which follows ISO 31000 methodology, so as to provide reasonable assurance that all significant climate risks are duly identified, evaluated, prevented and continuously monitored. Those risks are approved at the board level and managed in keeping with defined risk

## Page 170

tolerance thresholds.

## Page 170

The climate risk assessment contemplates transition and physical risks and also focuses on the opportunities that climate change could generate.

## Page 170

The accompanying chart depicts the classification of risks and opportunities considered in this process so that they can be assessed, including their financial impact, and subsequently managed and mitigated. The

## Page 170

process of measuring the financial impact of these risks is currently under development so as to cater to the needs and requirements brought to us by our stakeholders in recent years and those we expect to face in the coming years.
